payload.author: claude
payload.body:
PROPOSED MODEL (design-sweep result).

RATE LIMITING — two layers, reusing the {allowed, retryAfterMs} + 429/Retry-After shape already in pair.js: (1) per-principal token bucket keyed on auth.sub, mounted after authMiddleware (server.js:175) before workspace resolution — sized for fleets (e.g. 5 req/s burst 50) so a 6-agent fleet is fine but a runaway loop trips; acting-model is metadata, not a key, so agents share the user's bucket. (2) per-tenant sliding-window aggregate ceiling + hard quotas.

QUOTA DIMENSIONS (at the project=tenant boundary, counters alongside the PG event log per SCP-124): events/day (count only newly-applied rows on the ON CONFLICT upload), projects/account, seats, storage bytes, concurrent SSE connections (ceiling DERIVED FROM SCP-150's per-node benchmark so summed tenant ceilings stay within tested capacity). Surface via GET /api/meta + a usage panel; soft-warn at ~80% so agents back off gracefully.

ABUSE MODEL: realistic threat = a runaway agent in a loop (not a malicious attacker — auth gated by SCP-122). Containment: per-principal bucket -> per-tenant daily quota -> SSE reconnect storms bounded by the connection ceiling + Last-Event-ID resume (SCP-125). Sustained 429s -> temporary cooldown. All fail closed.

PRICING SHAPE — DECISION FOR BRI (flagged, not decided). Willingness=YES, unit OPEN (SCP-121). Options: (1) per-active-agent/usage-on-events — meters the actual value, matches the token-cost-avoided pitch, but harder to forecast; (2) per-human-seat — familiar/forecastable but under-captures a 1-human/10-agent fleet (the target buyer); (3) per-project tiered — simplest, matches 'project -> authenticate -> sync'. RECOMMENDATION: start with per-project tiers whose limits ARE the quota dimensions, with event-overage usage as a later lever (the per-event meter is already built in SCP-124, so option 1 stays available without re-architecting). Free tier: 1 project, ~2 seats, low events/day, a couple SSE connections. Billing: Stripe (Checkout + Portal + metered usage); webhook writes the plan row the quota checks read; entirely server-side, never touches the offline-first client path.

Deps: SCP-122 (claim shape, hard blocker), SCP-124 (event/storage counters + RLS), SCP-125/SCP-150 (connection ceiling). Gated behind multi-tenancy existing.

CHILD TICKETS: SCP-156 (rate-limit middleware), SCP-157 (quota enforcement + metering), SCP-158 (SSE connection ceiling), SCP-159 (surface usage to users), SCP-160 (Stripe + plan tiers).
